octaves._note: Reference bakes this as a compile-time #define / WGSL const (perf-only: lets the optimizer unroll the fbm4D octave loop). HLSL declares it as an int uniform 'OCTAVES' and bounds the loop at runtime ([loop]); values identical. Default 1 when unset.

colorMode._note: Reference bakes this as a compile-time #define / WGSL const (perf-only DCE of the RGB branch's two extra fbm4D calls). HLSL declares it as an int uniform 'COLOR_MODE' and branches at runtime. Default 0 (mono) when unset.

ridges._note: Reference bakes this as a compile-time #define / WGSL const (perf-only DCE of a per-octave branch). HLSL declares it as an int uniform 'RIDGES' (1/0) tested != 0 at runtime. Default 0 (false) when unset.

precompute._note: WGSL binds time/scale/seed/volumeSize/speed as separate uniform buffers (@binding 0..4), not a packed vec4 array. OCTAVES/COLOR_MODE/RIDGES are injected consts (compile-time) in the reference and are NOT in the buffer; in the HLSL port they are the int uniforms OCTAVES/COLOR_MODE/RIDGES set per frame. This slot map is illustrative; the HLSL backend sets all params as individual named uniforms by reference name.

passes.name: precompute
passes._role: VOLUME-WRITE (MRT, drawBuffers:2). Renders at the ATLAS viewport (volumeSize x volumeSize^2 = 64 x 4096 by default), NOT screen resolution. Fragment maps atlas pixel (x,y) -> voxel (x, y%volSize, y/volSize), writes noise density to SV_Target0 (color->volumeCache) and encoded normal+density to SV_Target1 (geoOut->geoBuffer). NM_FragCoord must yield atlas pixel coords (runtime binds _NM_Resolution = viewport/atlas size for this pass).

volumeCache._role: VOLUME atlas (vol-tier surface, reference 04 §8). 2D RenderTexture sized volumeSize x volumeSize^2 (default 64 x 4096 == 64 slices of 64x64), rgba16f LINEAR. Holds voxel density: COLOR_MODE 0 -> rgb=density, COLOR_MODE 1 -> r/g/b = three w-offset noise channels; a=1. Atlas (u,v) -> voxel (x = u, y = v%volSize, z = v/volSize). Exposed downstream as outputTex3d for render3d/renderLit3d raymarch. Width/height resolve from the volumeSize param (resolveDimension: param->64, param^2->4096; reference 04 §9).

geoBuffer._role: GEO atlas (geo-tier surface, reference 04 §8: xyz=normal, w=depth/density). Same atlas dims/format as volumeCache. Written by the precompute MRT (SV_Target1/geoOut): xyz = analytic surface normal encoded *0.5+0.5, w = noise density. Exposed downstream as outputGeo for lit raymarch. Out-of-bounds voxels write (0.5,0.5,0.5,0.0) = neutral normal, zero density.

render3d._comment: FIRST 3D / RENDER-TIER port. Single VOLUME-WRITE fullscreen pass with MRT (drawBuffers:2). No feedback, no geometry/scatter, no raymarch in THIS effect (it only fills the atlas; render3d/renderLit3d raymarchs it downstream). Atlas layout per reference 04 §8 / reference 10 §3: a 2D RenderTexture volumeSize x volumeSize^2 (default 64 x 4096), 64 slices of 64x64, rgba16f LINEAR. Atlas pixel->voxel mapping reproduced exactly: x = pixel.x, y = pixel.y % volSize, z = pixel.y / volSize (int % and /). The viewport for this pass is the atlas size, not the screen — the runtime must set the viewport AND _NM_Resolution to the atlas dimensions so NM_FragCoord recovers integer voxel addressing. OCTAVES/COLOR_MODE/RIDGES were compile-time injected consts in WGSL/GLSL (perf-only: unroll/DCE); the HLSL port models them as runtime int uniforms (PORTING-GUIDE 'Compile-time defines'). 'time' is normalized 0..1 (engine global); w = time*speed*W_PERIOD with W_PERIOD=4 gives a seamless time loop via lattice-level w wrap (nm_mod, NOT fmod). No '_iteration' uniform; no repeat.
render3d.voxelMapping: x = pixel.x; y = pixel.y % volSize; z = pixel.y / volSize
